description.en: Question 1. Which analog clock shows the time 7:08? Choices: A: the longer hand points between 1 and 2, and the shorter hand points just past 7; B: the longer hand points to 8, and the shorter hand is between 7 and 8; C: the longer hand points to 7, and the shorter hand is between 1 and 2; D: the longer hand points between 1 and 2, and the shorter hand points just past 8.
Question 2. What is 836 rounded to the nearest 10? Choices: A: 800; B: 830; C: 840; D: 870.
Question 3. Which two values are located at the same point on a number line? Choices: A: 4/1 and 4; B: 1/3 and 3; C: 8/8 and 8; D: 6/2 and 4.

Question 7. Umi created the number pattern below by adding the same amount each time to get the next number. 20, 40, 60, 80,... What will be the eighth number in the pattern? Choices: A: 160; B: 240; C: 320; D: 640.
Question 8. In which equation could the number six replace the question mark to make the equation true? Choices: A: 9 × □ = 56; B: 48 ÷ □ = 8; C: 30 × 5 = □; D: 24 ÷ 3 = □.

Question 12. A table titled Wheels Needed for Wagons shows: 1 wagon, 4 wheels; 2 wagons, 8 wheels; 3 wagons, 12 wheels; 4 wagons, 16 wheels. What is one pattern that can be seen in the table? Choices: A: The number of wheels increases by 1 each time; B: The number of wheels increases by 3 each time; C: The number of wheels increases by 4 each time; D: The number of wheels increases by 12 each time.
Question 13. If the equation 5 × □ = 45 is true, then which expression can be used to find the missing value? Choices: A: 9 ÷ 45; B: 5 ÷ 45; C: 45 ÷ 9; D: 45 ÷ 5.

Question 14. Keisha’s step-shaped poster board is composed of an upper rectangle measuring 8 inches by 3 inches and a lower rectangle measuring 8 inches by 4 inches. The rectangles meet along part of an edge and do not overlap. What was the total area, in square inches, of Keisha’s poster board? Choices: A: 46 square inches; B: 56 square inches; C: 112 square inches; D: 192 square inches.

Question 19. Maddie will ride her bike a total of 56 miles over 7 days. She will ride the same number of miles each day. What is the total number of miles Maddie will ride each day? Choices: A: 8; B: 9; C: 49; D: 63.

Question 20. Four students each drew a figure and shaded part to represent a fraction. Selena’s diamond is divided into 8 equal triangles with 5 shaded. Tara’s five-sided figure is divided into 2 equal vertical parts with 1 shaded. Carson’s triangle is divided into smaller triangular regions with the two bottom regions shaded. Erik’s octagon is divided into 8 triangular sectors with the bottom 3 shaded. Which sentence about the figures is true? Choices: A: Selena shaded 5/8 of her figure; B: Tara shaded 1/1 of her figure; C: Carson shaded 2/4 of his figure; D: Erik shaded 5/3 of his figure.

Question 21. A group of students played a basketball game after school. Which total can be found using the expression 7 × 2? Choices: A: the total number of points if a player made 7 shots and each shot was worth 2 points; B: the total number of basketballs if 7 basketballs were old and 2 basketballs were new; C: the total number of points if one player had 7 points and a different player had 2 points; D: the total number of basketballs used if there were 7 basketballs and 2 of the basketballs were not used.
Question 22. Which two fractions both represent the same location on a number line? Choices: A: 2/3 and 1/5; B: 3/4 and 6/8; C: 2/4 and 3/5; D: 5/6 and 5/8.
Question 23. What number makes the equation 81 ÷ □ = 9 true? Choices: A: 8; B: 9; C: 72; D: 90.
Question 24. Which expression is equal to 720? Choices: A: 7x20; B: 8x80; C: 9x80; D: 9x90.

Question 25. Mr. Kohlberg owns a flower shop. At the beginning of the day, he had 152 roses. Mr. Kohlberg sold 96 of the roses and then wanted to separate the rest of the roses equally among 8 vases. What will be the total number of roses in each vase? Choices: A: 7; B: 12; C: 48; D: 56.

Question 26. Which of four shapes is 2/3 shaded? Shape 1 is a triangle divided by horizontal lines into 3 unequal regions, with the middle and bottom regions shaded. Shape 2 is a square with a diagonal white band and 2 shaded corner regions. Shape 3 is a triangle divided into 3 equal regions, with the top and bottom-left regions shaded. Shape 4 is a rectangle whose left half is unshaded and whose right half is divided into 2 shaded regions. Choices: A: shape 1; B: shape 2; C: shape 3; D: shape 4.

Question 27. a7 Which situation could be represented by the expression 6 x 2? Choices: A: Rocco hiked six miles each day for two days; B: Rocco had six baseballs and gave away two of them; C: Rocco had a total of six tennis balls in two cans; D: Rocco biked six miles and then continued for two more miles.

Question 31. Mr. Gomez built a deck with an area of 29 square meters. The key says each small square equals 1 square meter. Which figure could represent the deck? Choices: A: a 4-by-7 rectangular grid; B: a figure with 3 rows of 10 squares and a centered 2-by-4 extension below; C: a 2-by-9 rectangular grid; D: an upside-down U-shaped figure with 7 squares in each of the top 3 rows and 4 squares in each of the bottom 2 rows.
Question 32. A number belongs in a box. When the number is rounded to the nearest hundred, the result will be 900. Which number belongs in the box? Choices: A: 849; B: 852; C: 960; D: 999.

Question 33. Kara has a bucket marked in 1-liter increments and labeled at 5 L, 10 L, and 15 L. The water level is at 12 liters. Kara wants to pour all of the water equally into 3 bowls for her dogs. How many liters of water should Kara pour into each bowl? Choices: A: 4; B: 5; C: 9; D: 15.

Question 34. Leroy made a diamond-shaped game board divided into 9 equal small squares arranged as a rotated 3-by-3 grid. The center square is shaded. What fraction of the game board is shaded? Choices: A: 1/9; B: 1/8; C: 1/6; D: 1/3.

Question 38. Tomas’s poster is shown as a shaded rectangle 6 unit squares wide and 4 unit squares high on a grid; the key says each square equals 1 square unit. Which figure has the same area as the poster? Choices: A: a rectangle measuring 8 units by 3 units; B: a square measuring 6 units by 6 units; C: a rectangle measuring 7 units by 3 units; D: a square measuring 5 units by 5 units.

Question 39. The first number in a number pattern is 28. The pattern rule is to add 14 to get the next number in the pattern. If the pattern continues, which statement is true? Choices: A: All the numbers in the pattern can be divided equally by 10; B: All the numbers in the pattern can be divided equally by 4; C: All the numbers in the pattern can be divided equally by 8; D: All the numbers in the pattern can be divided equally by 7.
Question 40. There were 6 rows of chairs set up for a meeting. Each row had 8 chairs. What was the total number of chairs set up for the meeting? Choices: A: 14; B: 36; C: 48; D: 64.

Question 41. A circle is divided into parts. Each part is 1/4 of the total area of the circle. Which sentence describes the circle? Choices: A: The circle has 1 small part and 3 large parts; B: The circle has 1 small part and 4 large parts; C: The circle has 4 parts that are each the same size; D: The circle has 5 parts that are each the same size.

Question 42. A baker made 232 muffins. He sent 190 of the muffins to a local hotel. He will put the rest of the muffins in boxes. Each box can hold 6 muffins. Which equation can be used to find b, the number of boxes the baker will need? Choices: A: (232 − 190) ÷ 6 = b; B: (232 + 190) × 6 = b; C: (232 − 190) × 6 = b; D: (232 + 190) ÷ 6 = b.
